|\          / \    version 3.16.0-ci20260327101+pr2099.bf5d4490b07add846805b9befc42cd0fc62a8e3b
| \        /   \   https://github.com/rnwood/smtp4dev
|  smtp4dev    /  .NET 10.0.5
|             /
└────────────'
[INF] smtp4dev version 3.16.0-ci20260327101+pr2099.bf5d4490b07add846805b9befc42cd0fc62a8e3b
[INF] https://github.com/rnwood/smtp4dev
[INF] .NET Core runtime version: .NET 10.0.5
[INF]
> For information about default values see documentation in appsettings.json.
-h, --help, -?             Shows this message and exits
--baseappdatapath=VALUE
Set the base config and appData path
--hostname=VALUE       Specifies the server hostname. Used in auto-
generated TLS certificate for SMTP if enabled.
--allowremoteconnections
Specifies if remote connections will be allowed to
the SMTP and IMAP servers. Use -
allowremoteconnections+ to enable or -
allowremoteconnections- to disable
--bindaddress=VALUE    Specifies the IP address to bind to for SMTP and
IMAP servers. If not specified, the behavior is
determined by allowremoteconnections
--disableipv6          If true, SMTP and IMAP servers will NOT listen
using IPv6 Dual Stack
--smtpport=VALUE       Set the port the SMTP server listens on. Specify 0
to assign automatically
--db=VALUE             Specifies the path where the database will be
stored relative to APPDATA env var on Windows or
XDG_CONFIG_HOME on non-Windows. Specify "" to
use an in memory database.
--messagestokeep=VALUE Specifies the number of messages to keep per
mailbox
--sessionstokeep=VALUE Specifies the number of sessions to keep
--tlsmode=VALUE        Specifies the TLS mode to use for SMTP only. (POP3
uses --pop3tlsmode). Valid options: None,
StartTls, ImplicitTls.
--tlscertificatestorethumbprint=VALUE
Specifies the thumbprint to find the certificate
from the computer's store to use for SMTP if TLS
is enabled/requested. This must be an X509.
Specify "" to use the path option, or an auto-
generated self-signed certificate (then see
console output on first startup).
--tlscertificate=VALUE Specifies the TLS certificate file to use for SMTP
if TLS is enabled/requested. This must be an
X509 certificate - generally a .CER, .CRT or .
PFX file. If using .CER or .CRT, you must
provide the private key separately using --
tlscertificateprivatekey.  Specify "" to use an
auto-generated self-signed certificate (then see
console output on first startup).
--tlscertificateprivatekey=VALUE
Specifies the corresponding private key file for
the SMTP TLS certificate if the private key is
not part of the TlsCertificate file
--tlscertificatepassword=VALUE
Specifies the password for  SMTP TLSCertificate/
TlsCertificatePrivateKey
--relaysmtpserver=VALUE
Sets the name of the SMTP server that will be used
to relay messages or "" if messages relay should
not be allowed
--relaysmtpport=VALUE  Sets the port number for the SMTP server used to
relay messages
--relayautomaticallyemails=VALUE
A comma separated list of recipient addresses for
which messages will be relayed automatically. An
empty list means that no messages are relayed
--relaysenderaddress=VALUE
Specifies the address used in MAIL FROM when
relaying messages. (Sender address in message
headers is left unmodified). The sender of each
message is used if not specified.
--relayusername=VALUE  The username for the SMTP server used to relay
messages. If "" no authentication is attempted
--relaypassword=VALUE  The password for the SMTP server used to relay
messages
--relaytlsmode=VALUE   Sets the TLS mode when connecting to relay SMTP
server. See: http://www.mimekit.net/docs/html/T_
MailKit_Security_SecureSocketOptions.htm
--imapport=VALUE       Specifies the port the IMAP server will listen on -
allows standard email clients to view/retrieve
messages. Specify --imapport="" to disable the
IMAP server.
--pop3port=VALUE       Specifies the port the POP3 server will listen on -
allows standard email clients to retrieve
messages. Specify --pop3port="" to disable the
POP3 server.
--pop3tlsmode=VALUE    Specifies the TLS mode for POP3 (None|StartTls|
ImplicitTls).
--pop3secureconnectionrequired
Require secure connection (TLS) for POP3 clients
--nousersettings       Skip loading of appsetttings.json file in %APPDATA%
--debugsettings        Prints out most settings values on startup
--recreatedb           Recreates the DB on startup if it already exists
--locksettings         Locks settings from being changed by user via web
interface
--installpath=VALUE    Sets path to folder containing wwwroot and other
files
--disablemessagesanitisation
Disables message HTML sanitisation.
--authenticationrequired
Requires that SMTP and IMAP clients authenticate
--secureconnectionrequired
Requires that SMTP clients use SSL/TLS
--smtpallowanycredentials
True if the SMTP server will allow any credentials
to be used without checking them again the '
Users'
--webauthenticationrequired
Require authentication for web interface
--delivermessagestousersdefaultmailbox
True if the mailbox recipient filter should be
ignored for authenticated smtp sessions and
messages should be delivered to the user's
default mailbox instead
--user=VALUE           Adds a user and password combination for web, SMTP
and IMAP. Use format username=password. This
option can be repeated to add multiple users.
--SmtpAuthTypesNotSecure=VALUE
SMTP auth type enabled when not using secure
connection (choices: ANONYMOUS, PLAIN, LOGIN,
CRAM-MD5). Separate values with comma.
--SmtpAuthTypesSecure=VALUE
SMTP auth type enabled when  using secure
connection (choices: ANONYMOUS, PLAIN, LOGIN,
CRAM-MD5). Separate values with comma.
--mailbox=VALUE        Adds a mailbox in Name=Recipients format or as
JSON. See appsettings.json for JSON format with
header/source filters. This option can be
repeated to add multiple mailboxes.
--sslprotocols=VALUE   Specifies the SSL/TLS protocol version(s) that
will be allowed. Separate with commas. See https:
//learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.
security.authentication.sslprotocols?view=net-9.0
--tlsciphersuites=VALUE
Specifies the TLS cipher suites to be allowed. Not
supported on Windows. Separate with commas. See
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/
system.net.security.tlsciphersuite?view=net-9.0
--HtmlValidateConfigfile=VALUE
Defines path to a config file used for HTML
validation. See https://html-validate.org/usage/
index.html#configuration
--maxmessagesize=VALUE Defines the maximum message size in bytes accepted
by the SMTP server
--tui                  Run with Terminal User Interface (TUI) instead of
web interface
--delivertostdout=VALUE
Specifies mailboxes (comma-separated) or '*' to
output received raw message content to stdout
--exitafter=VALUE      Specifies the number of messages to receive before
exiting the application (used with
delivertostdout)
--oauth2authority=VALUE
OAuth2/XOAUTH2 Identity Provider authority URL for
token validation (e.g., https://login.
microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0)
--oauth2audience=VALUE OAuth2/XOAUTH2 expected audience value for token
validation
--oauth2issuer=VALUE   OAuth2/XOAUTH2 expected issuer value for token
validation (optional, defaults to authority's
issuer)
--service              Required when running as a Windows service (
started by the Service Control Manager). To
install the service use --install-service.
--install-service      Installs smtp4dev as a Windows service and exits.
Requires administrator privileges. Windows only.
--uninstall-service    Uninstalls the smtp4dev Windows service and exits.
Requires administrator privileges. Windows only.
--urls=VALUE           The URLs the web interface should listen on. For
example, http://localhost:123. Use `*` in place
of hostname to listen for requests on any IP
address or hostname using the specified port and
protocol (for example, http://*:5000). Separate
multiple values with ;. For info about HTTPS see
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/
fundamentals/servers/kestrel/endpoints?view=
aspnetcore-8.0#configure-https-in-appsettingsjson
--basepath=VALUE       Specifies the virtual path from web server root
where SMTP4DEV web interface will be hosted. e.g.
"/" or "/smtp4dev"
